title: The framework, four sources, and the niyya hinge.
subtitle: We make the structure as visible as we can — knowing what the application asks (and what it does not) determines how you read its output.

fourSource.title: Four sources — co-necessary, asymmetrically ordered
fourSource.body: Knowledge and moral judgment draw on four sources: revelation (waḥy), reason (ʿaql), sense-experience (tajriba), and conscience (qalb / ḍamīr). None is sufficient on its own. Where they appear to conflict in content, the question is which side has the stronger warrant in the specific case — not a pre-set hierarchy but a public adjudication rule. Intention is the hinge at the moment any of the four becomes action.

maqasid.title: Five protected goods — al-Shāṭibī's skeleton
maqasid.body: Al-Shāṭibī shows by induction over the totality of revealed rulings that the Sharīʿa's commands track five protected goods: religion, life, intellect, lineage, property. They are not a ranked preference list but the structural conditions of human existence. The benefit calculus does not flatten these onto a single utilitarian scale — it tracks the impact on each.

tier.title: Three tiers — ḍarūrī / ḥājī / taḥsīnī
tier.body: Two impacts on the same axis are not equally weighty. Protecting a ḍarūrī (necessary) good, a ḥājī (needed) good, and a taḥsīnī (refining) good belong to different tiers. The algorithm weights every impact by its tier.

niyya.title: Niyya — a mirror, not a score
niyya.body: In the fifth step, the application asks five reflective questions. Your answers are not scored; they are reflected back. The algorithm does only one thing with them: if your stated direction conflicts with the option scoring highest, that tension is gently surfaced. Intention is neither sufficient on its own, nor ignorable.

commitments.title: Six falsifiable commitments
commitments.body: The framework is a testable program, not an attitude. The six commitments below define the thresholds at which the framework would refuse to keep operating. If any is shown to fail across a wide range of cases, the framework needs revision.

classification.title: Output classes
classification.body: The algorithm does not issue verdicts. It returns one of three classes.

fourSource.title: Four sources and the niyya hinge
fourSource.caption: Four co-necessary sources, asymmetrically ordered; at the moment of action, all four pass through the hinge of intention.

maqasid.caption: The classical five ḍarūriyyāt from al-Shāṭibī's al-Muwāfaqāt. The user assigns how each option affects each good; the application does not assign that for her.

tier.caption: The weights of impacts on the same maqsad differ by tier.

daruri.label: Ḍarūrī
daruri.desc: Necessities: without these, human life collapses into chaos — religion, life, intellect, lineage, property. Threats here override most other calculations.

haji.label: Ḥājī
haji.desc: Needs: not strictly indispensable but their absence creates significant hardship. The Sharīʿa's licit concessions (rukhṣas) operate at this level.

tahsini.label: Taḥsīnī
tahsini.desc: Refinements: the dignity, beauty, and excellence of human life. Preserved as long as the higher tiers are not breached.

c1.body: A judgment of moral rightness that ignores truth-conditions is defective.

c2.body: A truth-claim pressed into action with weak evidence must be marked ẓann, not yaqīn.

c3.body: Benefit assessment cannot define the good; it can only inform it.

c4.body: Intention is always morally relevant, never sufficient on its own.

c5.body: Source conflicts must be resolved by public rules, not charismatic intuition.

c6.body: Persistent qualified disagreement is itself a meaningful output, not an error state.

settled.title: High confidence
settled.body: Top option exceeds the runner-up clearly, sources converge, niyya is clean. Even so, consultation with a qualified scholar is recommended.

qualified.title: Reasonable divergence
qualified.body: Multiple options score within 20% of each other, or there is a known scholarly disagreement on the case. The framework does not pick for you; it presents the disagreement honestly.

open.title: Insufficient evidence
open.body: Critical claims at shakk level, literature is thin, or the facts are not yet settled. The framework structures the inquiry instead of issuing a verdict.
